div.head: The Minister in Finland (Schoenfeld) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 211. Finnish General Staff informs our Military Attaché this morning that German statement early today regarding alleged Finnish support of German attack on Soviet Union signifies that neither Finns nor Germans will take offensive against Russians on Finnish frontier and that Finnish support of Germans will be defensive. General Staff denied that there were any German troops in southern Finland as reported in my telegram 210, June 21, 2 p.m.Not printed. Huthsteiner was told that, according to information received by Finnish General Staff, Third Russian Army Corps was destroyed this morning in the Grodno area of Poland. General Staff is moving to field headquarters. It is not intended to blackout Helsinki for the present and the civil population remains calm.
While aboard a sailboat yesterday afternoon in Helsinki Bay, I personally saw two German E–boats enter the harbor.
